title: $totlines
text: $totlines Return the total number of lines available on the page for printing. $totlines Example: vLineNum = ($totlines - $lines) Return Values Table 1. Values returned in $totlines Value Meaning 0 Uniface is not printing ( $printing = 0); $status is set to an empty string ("") >0 Total number of lines available for printing, excluding the header and footer frames. Uniface is printing ( $printing = 1) Use Use in Form and Report components. Description The $totlines function is a useful way of logging how a report is formatted for print. For example, you might want to warn users if their occurrence has spread over two pages. Another use for $totlines is to calculate the current line number. Example: Calculating the Current Line Number The following example uses $totlines and $lines to calculate the current line number: vLineNum = ($totlines - $lines) Copy Example: Checking if Entity Fits on Page The following example uses $totlines to check whether an entity can fit on a page, and prints a message if it cannot fit on the page: trigger LeavePrinted if (($totlines - $framedepth) < 0) putmess "%%$entname didn't fit on 1 page: see page %%$page." endif end Copy Related Links $lines Return the number of lines remaining on the current page. Printing Character Sets To print, choose the device translation table that is specific for the character set. $framedepth Return the number of lines needed to print a frame as drawn on the component, without allowing for expansion $printing Return a status indicating whether the current component is printing.
